applicant_information.address: 7420 Brookside Commons Dr, Suite 210, Indianapolis, IN 46250

current_uses.describe_the_current_uses_of_the_propert:
The subject properties at 1450 and 1460 E 62nd St are currently improved with a one-story masonry commercial building and associated off-street parking that function together as a small neighborhood retail and service center. The primary structure spans both parcels, with the interior demising walls and utilities organized to allow for multiple small tenant spaces under a single roof. As of the date of this application, the building is occupied by three tenants with complementary, low-intensity commercial uses that primarily serve the surrounding residential neighborhoods within a roughly two-mile radius.

On the western portion of the building (addressed as 1450 E 62nd St, Suite A), there is a locally owned coffee shop and café, "Canal View Coffee," which operates from approximately 6:30 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Monday through Friday and 7:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. on weekends. The café offers counter-service coffee, tea, and light food items (such as pastries, sandwiches, and salads) for on-premise consumption and carryout. Seating is provided indoors for approximately 24 patrons, with an additional 8 seasonal outdoor seats on a small patio area along the front façade. No drive-through or amplified outdoor music is provided. Deliveries are limited to small box trucks and vans and generally occur during weekday mornings between 7:00 a.m. and 10:00 a.m. Trash and recycling are stored in screened dumpsters at the rear of the building, which are shared with the other tenants.

In the central portion of the building (1450 E 62nd St, Suite B), there is a neighborhood-oriented professional office user, "Northside Tax & Financial Services." This tenant provides individual and small-business tax preparation, bookkeeping, and general financial consulting. Office hours are typically 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m., Monday through Friday, with extended hours during the January–April tax season and occasional Saturday appointments by request. Most client interactions occur by scheduled appointment, resulting in a relatively steady but modest flow of customers and staff vehicles. There are no large meetings, classes, or events hosted on-site, and no outdoor operations of any kind.

On the eastern portion of the building (addressed as 1460 E 62nd St, Suite C), the current user is a small personal services tenant, "Lotus Physical Therapy & Wellness Studio." This business provides one-on-one and small-group physical therapy and wellness sessions, focusing primarily on post-injury rehabilitation, mobility training, and preventative care. Hours are generally 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Monday through Thursday and 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. on Friday, with occasional early-morning or early-evening appointments to accommodate client schedules. The studio includes a small open exercise area, treatment rooms, a reception area, and a staff office. Group classes are limited to 6–8 participants at a time, which keeps parking demand and noise levels low.

The site as a whole includes a shared surface parking lot with approximately 28 striped spaces, including two accessible spaces located near the main entrance, and a modest amount of landscaping along the front and side property lines. Vehicular access is provided from E 62nd St via two existing curb cuts that have been in place for several decades. There is no direct vehicular access to adjoining residential streets. Pedestrian access is provided by a sidewalk connection from the public sidewalk on E 62nd St to the main building entrance. Exterior site lighting is provided by wall-mounted fixtures and two pole lights in the parking lot; all fixtures are downcast and shielded to minimize glare onto adjacent properties.

The immediate surroundings are a mix of small-scale commercial uses along E 62nd St, including a hair salon and a small law office, transitioning quickly to single-family residential neighborhoods to the north and south. The current uses are fully consistent with the historic character and intent of the C-3 Neighborhood Commercial District, providing small-scale, neighborhood-serving retail and service uses with limited hours, minimal noise, and modest traffic generation. No heavy commercial, industrial, or high-intensity entertainment uses are present on the site. There are no short-term rentals, residential dwelling units, or outdoor storage uses on the property. All operations, aside from the small seasonal café patio, are conducted entirely indoors.

The purpose of this rebuild letter request is to confirm that, in the event of damage or destruction by fire, storm, or other casualty, the existing building and its neighborhood-scale commercial use pattern may be reconstructed on the existing foundation footprint (or substantially similar footprint) and that the current mix of low-intensity retail, office, and personal service tenants may continue to operate in accordance with the applicable zoning regulations. This confirmation is being requested in connection with a refinancing and insurance policy renewal for the property, both of which have conditions related to the ability to rebuild and continue the current lawful uses should a catastrophic event occur.
current_uses.if_yes_describe_variance_special_use_exc: In 2003, the prior owner obtained a variance of development standards (Case #V-03-118) to allow a reduced front yard setback along E 62nd St in order to enclose an existing non-conforming canopy and expand the building footprint slightly toward the street while maintaining compliance with sight-distance and utility easement requirements.

As part of the same approval, the Board of Zoning Appeals granted a variance to permit a parking ratio of 1 space per 225 square feet of gross floor area (rather than the then-required 1 space per 200 square feet), based on evidence that the mix of neighborhood-scale office, retail, and personal service uses would generate lower peak parking demand than the ordinance assumed.

Conditions of approval included maintaining the existing landscaping along the north property line, limiting hours of operation for retail and personal service uses to 6:00 a.m.–10:00 p.m., and prohibiting drive-through facilities and outdoor amplified sound; these conditions remain in effect today and have been consistently observed by the current owner and tenants.
